sections.title: Section 1 - Introduction
sections.summary: Purpose, responsibilities, and how to use this handbook in training and field operations.

topics.title: Purpose of Handbook
topics.content: This handbook standardizes MCPD paperwork and reporting expectations. Use it as a field reference and training guide.

topics.title: Officer Responsibilities
topics.content: Maintain scene safety, preserve evidence, document facts objectively, and complete required forms before end of shift unless directed otherwise.

topics.title: How To Use This Guide
topics.content: Use the table of contents and search. Start with scenario guides for incident type, then use form-specific instructions.

sections.title: Section 2 - Report Writing Basics
sections.summary: Narrative quality standards and common mistakes to avoid.

topics.title: Narrative Structure
topics.content: Use chronological order: dispatch, arrival, observations, statements, actions taken, evidence collected, disposition.

topics.title: Clarity and Objectivity
topics.content: Write observed facts and attributed statements. Avoid assumptions and unexplained abbreviations.

topics.title: Common Mistakes
topics.content: Missing times, no legal basis language, undefined acronyms, no evidence chain details, and inconsistent person identifiers.

sections.title: Section 3 - Forms Overview
sections.summary: When each form is used and what information is required.

topics.title: MCPD Stat Sheet
topics.content: Shift activity and operational totals. Complete all count fields, role assignments, and narrative follow-up notes.

topics.title: Incident Report
topics.content: Primary incident documentation. Required for criminal and significant non-criminal events requiring official record.

topics.title: Witness Statement
topics.content: Captures witness narrative and contact details. Confirm legibility and statement attribution.

topics.title: Evidence / Property Forms
topics.content: Use for collection, packaging, transfer, and disposition tracking.

topics.title: Vehicle Impound Form
topics.content: Required when vehicle is impounded or towed under MCPD authority.

topics.title: Use of Force Report
topics.content: Required whenever force option beyond compliant escort is used.

topics.title: Supplemental Report
topics.content: Use for additional details after initial report submission.

sections.title: Section 4 - Scenario-Based Paperwork Guide
sections.summary: Baseline paperwork expectations by common incident type.

scenarios.title: Domestic Disturbance
scenarios.officer_responsibilities: Separate parties, assess injuries, identify primary aggressor, document victim/suspect statements, and notify supervisor as required.
scenarios.notes: Additional protective order or command notifications may be required.

scenarios.title: Shoplifting
scenarios.description: Theft from PX/retail or similar facility.
scenarios.officer_responsibilities: Preserve video references, inventory recovered property, and document value/owner confirmation.
scenarios.notes: Juvenile subjects require additional juvenile processing paperwork.

scenarios.title: Traffic Accident
scenarios.officer_responsibilities: Scene safety, injuries/medical actions, roadway factors, driver info, witness capture, and photos.
scenarios.notes: Additional citations or criminal forms may be required.

scenarios.title: Suspicious Person
scenarios.description: Behavior indicating potential threat or criminal activity.
scenarios.officer_responsibilities: Document articulable facts, actions taken, and disposition.
scenarios.notes: If evidence or contraband is found, add evidence forms.

scenarios.title: Theft
scenarios.officer_responsibilities: Document ownership, value, suspect access/opportunity, and evidence chain.
scenarios.notes: Add supplemental report if follow-up develops additional leads.

scenarios.title: Assault
scenarios.officer_responsibilities: Document injuries, photographs, statements, and suspect disposition.
scenarios.notes: Use of force report may be required depending on arrest resistance.

scenarios.title: Drug Possession
scenarios.description: Controlled substance seizure or possession allegation.
scenarios.officer_responsibilities: Secure and package evidence correctly with clear chain-of-custody details.
scenarios.notes: Lab submission references may be required.

scenarios.title: Lost Property
scenarios.officer_responsibilities: Collect detailed property descriptors and owner contact information.
scenarios.notes: If later recovered, add found/recovered supplemental updates.

scenarios.title: Found Property
scenarios.officer_responsibilities: Document finder information and transfer into secure custody.

scenarios.title: Vehicle Impound
scenarios.description: Vehicle held/towed under legal or policy authority.
scenarios.officer_responsibilities: Document legal basis, inventory, tow details, and destination.

scenarios.title: Juvenile Incident
scenarios.officer_responsibilities: Follow juvenile notification and custody procedures.

scenarios.title: Trespass After Warning
scenarios.officer_responsibilities: Document prior warning details and jurisdictional authority.
scenarios.notes: Include location access restrictions and witness corroboration.

sections.title: Section 5 - Form Completion Guides
sections.summary: Field-by-field guidance and common mistakes for each major form.

form_guides.purpose: Track officer shift production, activity totals, and follow-up requirements.
field_instructions: Complete reporting period dates and shift.
Enter count totals as numbers only.
Use role entries to identify victim/suspect/witness context when tied to notable incidents.
Document follow-up actions in narrative fields.
example_values: Notable Incidents: Domestic disturbance and shoplifting arrest.
common_mistakes: Mixing narrative text inside numeric fields
Missing follow-up notes for pending actions
form_guides.correct_example_summary: All required reporting fields complete, counts validated, and narrative clearly tied to actions.

form_guides.purpose: Primary case documentation for incidents requiring official record.
form_guides.when_used: Criminal incidents and significant service calls.
field_instructions: Use full names/identifiers for all involved persons.
Capture times, location, legal basis, and actions taken.
example_values: Disposition: Suspect apprehended and transferred for processing.
form_guides.correct_example_summary: Narrative is factual, chronological, and supported by evidence references.

sections.title: Appendix A - Blank Forms
sections.summary: Blank printable forms for manual use when needed.
